text.title: handoff-id-payload Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for handoff-id-payload. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The handoff payload from agent A to agent B MUST be a structured object with at minimum: task_id (string, key into the shared store), target_agent (string, role name), decision_metadata (object, the small set of facts B needs to plan its first action). It MUST NOT contain the raw user input, the conversation history, or A's reasoning.
rule.rationale: If B needs the input, it loads from the store using task_id. Forwarding the conversation forces every agent to read the cumulative trace, which scales O(n²) in chain length.

rule.statement: Supervisor agents that route between workers MUST return a SupervisorDecision object (target_agent, reason, decision_metadata) as structured output — NOT a free-form message naming the next worker.
rule.rationale: Free-form routing is fragile (model picks a worker name that doesn't exist, the orchestrator has to re-prompt). Structured output makes routing a typed dispatch.

rule.statement: The receiving agent MUST load task state from the shared store on first action — not from the handoff payload. The payload's decision_metadata is for routing decisions only, not for reconstructing inputs.
rule.rationale: Single source of truth. If the metadata and the store disagree, the store wins; otherwise duplication causes drift between what the agent thinks the task is and what it actually is.

rule.statement: The store MUST expose at least three operations: get(task_id) → state, append_event(task_id, event), set_status(task_id, status). State updates MUST be append-only events, never overwrites of prior fields, so a downstream agent or a re-run can replay history.
rule.rationale: Append-only state lets a failed handoff be retried without losing the prior agent's output; overwrite stores destroy evidence on retry.
